from app.ai.stages import detect_stage, VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED
import pytest

def test_empty_input_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(iter([])) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None

def test_latest_message_negating_pattern_triggers_viewing_discussion(monkeypatch):
    # Patch NEGATING_PATTERNS to include a pattern matching "nope"
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.NEGATING_PATTERNS", [r"nope"])
    messages = [{"message": "Nope, not interested"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_rearrange_alias_triggers_viewing_discussion(monkeypatch):
    # The exact patterns are fixed in spec, test one alias "rearrange"
    messages = [{"message": "Can we rearrange the appointment?"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_fallback_to_content_key_and_case_insensitive_matching(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.NEGATING_PATTERNS", [r"cancel"])
    messages = [
        {"content": "Please CANCEL my booking"},
        {"message": None},
        {"content": ""},
        {"message": "irrelevant"},
    ]
    # Latest message is "irrelevant" (no negation), so None expected
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None
    # But if latest message contains negation pattern (case-insensitive)
    messages[-1]["message"] = "I want to cancel"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_only_last_8_messages_considered(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.NEGATING_PATTERNS", [r"nope"])
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered
    messages = [{"message": "nope"}] * 2 + [{"message": "irrelevant"}] * 8
    # Latest message is "irrelevant" (no negation), so None expected
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None
    # Change 9th message (first of last 8) to negation pattern, but latest message no
    messages[2]["message"] = "Nope"
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None
    # Change latest message to negation pattern triggers VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    messages[-1]["message"] = "Nope"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_booking_context_messages_trigger_viewing_booked(monkeypatch):
    # Patch BOOKED_PATTERNS and _has_time to simulate booking detection
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.BOOKED_PATTERNS", [r"confirmed"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages._has_time", lambda text: "10am" in text)
    messages = [
        {"message": "Your booking is confirmed for 10am"},
        {"message": "See you then"},
        {"message": "confirmed"},
        {"message": "10am appointment"},
        {"message": "irrelevant"},
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages considered, last 4 booking context messages combined contain booked pattern and time
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

import re
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude

# Since LANDLORD_ATTITUDES is unspecified, define a minimal set for testing
LANDLORD_ATTITUDES = {"aggressive", "suspicious", "helpful", "friendly", "cold", "slow_reply", "responsive"}

# Patch the module-level constants and patterns for testing
import app.ai.conversation_memory as conv_mem

conv_mem.LANDLORD_ATTITUDES = LANDLORD_ATTITUDES

# Define simple regex patterns to trigger each attitude for testing
conv_mem.AGGRESSIVE_PATTERNS = [re.compile(r"angry", re.I)]
conv_mem.SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS = [re.compile(r"suspect", re.I)]
conv_mem.HELPFUL_PATTERNS = [re.compile(r"help", re.I)]
conv_mem.FRIENDLY_PATTERNS = [re.compile(r"hello", re.I)]
conv_mem.COLD_PATTERNS = [re.compile(r"cold", re.I)]


def make_message(sender_label, content=None, created_at=None, message_key="message"):
    """Helper to create a message dict with given sender and content keys."""
    msg = {}
    if content is not None:
        msg[message_key] = content
    if created_at is not None:
        msg["created_at"] = created_at
    # We simulate _sender by monkeypatching _sender function to recognize sender_label
    # But since we can't patch private helpers, we simulate by monkeypatching _sender in conv_mem
    # Instead, we will monkeypatch _sender via pytest monkeypatch fixture in tests that need it.
    # Here just return dict with a special key to identify sender for monkeypatching.
    msg["_test_sender"] = sender_label
    return msg


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_sender(monkeypatch):
    # Patch _sender to read from _test_sender key for testing
    def _sender(message):
        return message.get("_test_sender", "")
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "_sender", _sender)
    # Patch _content to use spec: message.get("message") or message.get("content") or ""
    def _content(message):
        return str(message.get("message") or message.get("content") or "")
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "_content", _content)
    yield


def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_if_valid_else_responsive():
    # No messages, previous in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="friendly") == "friendly"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(None, previous="cold") == "cold"
    # previous not in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES returns "responsive"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="unknown") == "responsive"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(None, previous=None) == "responsive"


def test_landlord_messages_aggresive_pattern_triggers_aggressive():
    msgs = [
        make_message("tenant", "hello"),  # ignored sender
        make_message("landlord", "I am very angry about this!"),
        make_message("landlord", "Please fix it."),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "aggressive"


def test_landlord_messages_suspicious_pattern_triggers_suspicious_if_no_aggressive():
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "This looks suspect."),
        make_message("landlord", "I hope you understand."),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "suspicious"


def test_landlord_messages_helpful_pattern_triggers_helpful_if_no_higher_priority():
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "How can I help you?"),
        make_message("landlord", "Let me know."),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "helpful"


def test_landlord_messages_friendly_pattern_triggers_friendly_if_no_higher_priority():
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "Hello there!"),
        make_message("landlord", "Hope you are well."),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "friendly"


def test_landlord_messages_cold_pattern_triggers_cold_if_no_higher_priority():
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "This is cold."),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "cold"


def test_slow_reply_triggered_by_time_difference_over_24h():
    now = datetime.now()
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "Hello", created_at=now - timedelta(days=2)),
        make_message("landlord", "No rush", created_at=now),
    ]
    # No pattern matched, but time diff > 24h → slow_reply
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "slow_reply"


def test_slow_reply_not_triggered_if_only_one_landlord_message():
    now = datetime.now()
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "Hello", created_at=now - timedelta(days=2)),
    ]
    # Only one landlord message → no slow_reply check → responsive
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "responsive"


def test_slow_reply_not_triggered_if_created_at_missing_or_invalid():
    now = datetime.now()
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "Hello", created_at=now - timedelta(days=2)),
        {"_test_sender": "landlord", "message": "No rush"},  # no created_at
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "responsive"
    # created_at invalid type
    msgs_invalid = [
        make_message("landlord", "Hello", created_at=now - timedelta(days=2)),
        {"_test_sender": "landlord", "message": "No rush", "created_at": "not a datetime"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs_invalid) == "responsive"


def test_content_key_fallback_and_case_insensitivity():
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", None, message_key="message"),  # empty content
        make_message("landlord", "HeLLo", message_key="content"),  # fallback key
    ]
    # "hello" triggers friendly pattern (case-insensitive)
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "friendly"


def test_only_last_4_landlord_messages_considered_for_pattern_matching():
    # 5 landlord messages, only last 4 considered
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "angry"),  # 1st message, aggressive pattern but ignored
        make_message("landlord", "hello"),  # 2nd message, friendly
        make_message("landlord", "help"),   # 3rd message, helpful
        make_message("landlord", "suspect"),# 4th message, suspicious
        make_message("landlord", "friendly"),# 5th message, no pattern
    ]
    # aggressive pattern only in 1st message which is outside last 4 → ignored
    # suspicious pattern in 4th message → suspicious has higher priority than helpful/friendly
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "suspicious"


def test_messages_with_sender_alias_inbound_are_considered_landlord():
    msgs = [
        make_message("inbound", "I am angry!"),
        make_message("tenant", "hello"),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "aggressive"


def test_multiple_patterns_match_priority_order():
    # Messages contain both aggressive and suspicious patterns
    msgs = [
        make_message("landlord", "This is angry and suspect."),
    ]
    # aggressive has priority over suspicious
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs) == "aggressive"


def test_no_landlord_messages_and_previous_not_in_landlord_attitudes_returns_responsive():
    msgs = [
        make_message("tenant", "hello"),
        make_message("agent", "help"),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(msgs, previous="not_in_set") == "responsive"

import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import viewing_requested

def test_none_and_empty_messages_return_false():
    assert viewing_requested(None) is False
    assert viewing_requested([]) is False

def test_messages_with_no_relevant_keys_return_false():
    messages = [{"other": "no relevant keys"}, {"foo": "bar"}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is False

def test_message_key_preferred_over_content_and_falsy_values_treated_as_empty():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Is the viewing available?"},  # matches "viewing" and "available"
        {"message": "" , "content": "come round tomorrow?"},  # message is empty string, so content used
        {"content": None},  # falsy content treated as empty string
        {"message": None, "content": "I want to see it."}  # message falsy, content used
    ]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

def test_case_insensitive_and_whole_word_matching():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I want to VIEW it."},  # matches "view" case-insensitive
        {"message": "Looking forward to the Appointment."},  # matches "appointment"
        {"message": "I viewed the house."}  # "viewed" should NOT match "view"
    ]
    # The combined text contains "view" and "appointment" as whole words, so True
    # "viewed" does not cause a match
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

def test_multi_word_keywords_match_exact_phrases():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Can I come round tomorrow?"},  # matches "come round"
        {"message": "Would you like to come over later?"},  # matches "come over"
        {"message": "I want to see it."},  # matches "see it"
        {"message": "I want to see it now."},  # matches "see it"
        {"message": "I want to see it."}
    ]
    # Each individually matches, test them separately
    assert viewing_requested([{"message": "Can I come round tomorrow?"}]) is True
    assert viewing_requested([{"message": "Would you like to come over later?"}]) is True
    assert viewing_requested([{"message": "I want to see it."}]) is True

def test_partial_word_substrings_do_not_match():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I viewed the house."},  # "viewed" should not match "view"
        {"message": "Availability is limited."},  # "Availability" should not match "available"
        {"message": "The appointmented time is set."}  # "appointmented" should not match "appointment"
    ]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is False

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage, VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED
import pytest

def test_empty_and_none_input_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None

def test_latest_message_with_rearrange_returns_viewing_discussion():
    messages = [{"message": "Can we rearrange the appointment?"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_with_negating_pattern_returns_viewing_discussion():
    # Assuming negating pattern includes "not available" somewhere in text
    messages = [{"message": "I am NOT available tomorrow"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_fallback_to_content_key_and_case_insensitivity():
    messages = [{"content": "Please RESCHEDULE the meeting"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_only_last_8_messages_considered_and_order_preserved():
    # 9 messages, only last 8 considered, latest message triggers viewing discussion
    messages = [{"message": f"msg{i}"} for i in range(1, 10)]
    messages[-1]["message"] = "need to change the time"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_booking_context_messages_with_time_and_booked_pattern_returns_viewing_booked():
    # We simulate booking context messages with BOOKED_PATTERNS and time expression
    # Since exact patterns unknown, use a message that likely matches booking pattern and time
    messages = [
        {"message": "Your appointment is confirmed at 3pm"},
        {"message": "See you at 15:00"},
        {"message": "Booking confirmed"},
        {"message": "Looking forward to your visit at 2pm"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

def test_combined_recent_messages_matching_discussion_patterns_returns_viewing_discussion():
    # Assuming "discussion" word triggers discussion pattern somewhere in combined text
    messages = [
        {"message": "Hello"},
        {"message": "Let's have a discussion about the schedule"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_none_and_empty_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_exact_keyword_matching_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "OUTBOUND"},
        {"sender": "Ai"},
        {"sender": "bot"},
        {"sender": "user1"},
        {"sender": "user "},
        {"sender": " user"},
        {"sender": "user\n"},
    ]
    # Only exact matches count, ignoring case
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_fallback_to_direction_and_missing_keys():
    messages = [
        {"sender": None, "direction": "User"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "", "direction": "AI"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": None},
        {},
        {"sender": False, "direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": ""},
        {"sender": None},
    ]
    # First three count, last five do not
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_non_matching_senders_do_not_count():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "admin"},
        {"sender": "userbot"},
        {"sender": "outbounded"},
        {"sender": "ai assistant"},
        {"sender": "tenant123"},
        {"sender": "USER "},  # trailing space, no exact match
        {"direction": "user1"},
    ]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 0

def test_mixed_valid_and_invalid_messages():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "bot"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": ""},
        {"direction": None},
        {"sender": "AI"},
        {"sender": "unknown"},
    ]
    # Valid: User, outbound, tenant, AI
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_empty_string_and_falsy_values_treated_as_empty_sender():
    messages = [
        {"sender": ""},
        {"sender": None},
        {"sender": False},
        {"direction": ""},
        {"direction": None},
        {"direction": False},
        {},
    ]
    # All treated as empty string sender, none match keywords
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 0

import pytest
from types import SimpleNamespace
from app.ai.conversation_memory import phone_shared_state

def test_returns_true_if_conversation_phone_number_shared_at_truthy():
    conversation = SimpleNamespace(phone_number_shared_at=True)
    result = phone_shared_state([], None, conversation)
    assert result is True

    conversation = SimpleNamespace(phone_number_shared_at=123)
    result = phone_shared_state([], None, conversation)
    assert result is True

def test_returns_false_if_no_conversation_and_no_mobile_number():
    # persona is None, no mobile_number
    result = phone_shared_state([], None, None)
    assert result is False

    # persona with mobile_number None
    result = phone_shared_state([], {"mobile_number": None}, None)
    assert result is False

    # persona with empty mobile_number string
    result = phone_shared_state([], {"mobile_number": ""}, None)
    assert result is False

def test_returns_true_if_message_contains_local_number_match_case_insensitive_sender():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "+44 7123 456789"}
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User", "message": "Call me on 07123456789"},
        {"sender": "unknown", "message": "07123456789"},  # ignored sender
    ]
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert result is True

def test_returns_true_if_message_contains_compact_number_match_and_direction_used_as_sender():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "07123456789"}
    messages = [
        {"direction": "tenant", "content": "My number is 07123456789"},
        {"sender": "system", "message": "07123456789"},  # ignored sender
    ]
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert result is True

def test_skips_messages_with_unrecognized_sender_and_handles_missing_keys_and_none_values():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "07123456789"}
    messages = [
        {"sender": "unknown", "message": "07123456789"},  # skipped sender
        {"direction": None, "content": None},  # fallback to empty strings, no match
        {},  # no keys, fallback to empty strings, no match
        {"sender": None, "message": None},  # fallback to empty strings, no match
    ]
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert result is False

def test_returns_false_if_no_messages_and_mobile_number_present():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "07123456789"}
    result = phone_shared_state([], persona)
    assert result is False

    result = phone_shared_state(None, persona)
    assert result is False

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage, VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED
import pytest

def test_empty_input_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(iter([])) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None

def test_latest_message_rearrange_triggers_viewing_discussion():
    messages = [{"message": "Can we rearrange the appointment?"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_negating_pattern_triggers_viewing_discussion():
    # Assuming negating pattern includes "not going"
    messages = [{"message": "I am NOT going to make it"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_fallback_to_content_key_and_case_insensitive():
    messages = [{"content": "Please RESCHEDULE for another time"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_only_last_8_messages_are_considered_and_order_preserved():
    # 9 messages, only last 8 considered, latest triggers VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    messages = [{"message": f"msg{i}"} for i in range(1, 9)]
    messages.insert(0, {"message": "rearrange"})  # 9th message at front, ignored
    # latest message is "msg8" which does not trigger, so None expected
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

    # Now put rearrange in last message
    messages[-1] = {"message": "Let's rearrange this"}
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_booking_context_messages_trigger_viewing_booked():
    # Assuming BOOKED_PATTERNS includes "confirmed"
    # and _has_time detects "10:00am"
    messages = [
        {"message": "Your appointment is confirmed"},
        {"message": "See you at 10:00am"},
        {"message": "Looking forward to it"},
        {"message": "Thanks!"}
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

def test_discussion_patterns_in_combined_recent_messages_triggers_viewing_discussion():
    # Assuming DISCUSSION_PATTERNS includes "question"
    messages = [
        {"message": "I have a question about the booking"},
        {"message": "Please help"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

import datetime
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_empty_messages_returns_none():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_fallback_to_content_if_message_missing_and_empty_string_if_both_missing():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"content": "Booked for 3pm"},  # should pick content
        {"other": "ignored"},          # no message or content, treated as empty string
        {"message": None, "content": None},  # both None, treated as empty string
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(result, datetime.datetime)
    # The only valid time is in first message, 3pm -> 15:00 on now.date()
    expected = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 15, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_considers_only_last_8_messages_in_order_and_last_candidate_chosen():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 1pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 2pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 3pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 4pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 5pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 6pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 7pm"},  # ignored, too old
        {"message": "Booked for 8pm"},  # considered, candidate 1
        {"message": "Booked for 9pm and 10pm"},  # considered, candidate 2 (last candidate)
        {"message": "Booked for 11pm"},  # considered, candidate 3 (last candidate)
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # last candidate is 11pm on now.date()
    expected = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 23, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_time_suffix_and_hour_adjustments():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)
    # test am/pm and no suffix hour adjustments
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 12am"},  # hour=12 am -> 0
        {"message": "Booked for 1am"},   # hour=1 am no change
        {"message": "Booked for 12pm"},  # hour=12 pm no change
        {"message": "Booked for 3pm"},   # hour=3 pm +12=15
        {"message": "Booked for 4"},     # no suffix, hour=4 -> 16
        {"message": "Booked for 7"},     # no suffix, hour=7 -> 19
        {"message": "Booked for 8"},     # no suffix, hour=8 no change
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages considered, so all except first message
    # last candidate is "Booked for 8" with hour=8 no suffix no change
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 8, 0)
    # But candidate datetime < now (8:00 < 10:00), so one day added
    expected += datetime.timedelta(days=1)
    assert result == expected

def test_target_date_keywords_and_numeric_date_parsing():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)  # Saturday
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 3pm day after tomorrow"},  # now + 2 days = June 3
        {"message": "Booked for 4pm tomorrow"},            # now + 1 day = June 2
        {"message": "Booked for 5pm today"},               # now date = June 1
        {"message": "Booked for 6pm 2/6"},                 # numeric date 2 June (past), year missing -> next year 2025
        {"message": "Booked for 7pm 3/6/2024"},            # numeric date 3 June 2024 (future)
        {"message": "Booked for 8pm monday"},              # next Monday after June 1 is June 3
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages considered, all here
    # Last candidate is "Booked for 8pm monday" -> next Monday June 3
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected_date = datetime.date(2024, 6, 3)  # Monday
    expected = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(20, 0))
    assert result == expected

def test_no_qualifying_time_returns_none():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)
    messages = [
        {"message": "No time here"},
        {"content": "Still no time"},
        {"message": "Random text"},
    ]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) is None

import datetime
import re
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_empty_messages_returns_none():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_fallback_to_content_key_and_empty_string_for_missing_keys():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"irrelevant": "no message or content"},
        {"content": "Booked for 3pm today"},
        {"message": None, "content": "Discussion at 4pm tomorrow"},
        {"message": "Viewing at 5pm day after tomorrow"},
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # Last qualifying message is the 4th, time 5pm, day after tomorrow = now + 2 days
    expected_date = (now + datetime.timedelta(days=2)).date()
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(17, 0))
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_considers_only_last_8_messages_in_order():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered
    messages = []
    for i in range(10):
        # Messages 0-7: no time, messages 8 and 9 have times
        if i < 8:
            messages.append({"message": f"irrelevant message {i}"})
        else:
            messages.append({"message": f"Booked for {3+i}pm today"})
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # Only messages 2..9 considered, last qualifying is message 9 with time 12pm (3+9=12)
    expected_date = now.date()
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(21, 0))  # 12pm + 12 if no suffix? But suffix is pm, so 12pm is 12:00
    # Actually 12pm is 12:00, no addition needed
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(12, 0))
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_time_suffix_and_hour_adjustments():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 12am today"},  # hour=12, am -> hour=0
        {"message": "Booked for 3 today"},     # no suffix, hour=3 -> 15
        {"message": "Booked for 7pm today"},   # pm and hour<12 -> hour+12=19
        {"message": "Booked for 11am today"},  # am and hour!=12 -> hour=11
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages considered, all 4 here, last qualifying is last message with 11am
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected_date = now.date()
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(11, 0))
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_target_date_parsing_with_keywords_and_numeric_date(monkeypatch):
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)  # Saturday
    messages = [
        {"message": "Viewing at 3pm day after tomorrow"},
        {"message": "Viewing at 4pm tomorrow"},
        {"message": "Viewing at 5pm today"},
        {"message": "Viewing at 6pm 2/6"},  # numeric date 2 June (past date relative to now)
        {"message": "Viewing at 7pm 3/6/24"},  # numeric date 3 June 2024
        {"message": "Viewing at 8pm monday"},
    ]
    # Last qualifying message is last one with "monday"
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # Next Monday after 1 June 2024 (Saturday) is 3 June 2024
    expected_date = datetime.date(2024, 6, 3)
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(20, 0))
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_candidate_datetime_before_now_rolls_forward_one_day():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 18, 0)
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booked for 3pm today"},  # 15:00 today < now 18:00
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected_date = (now + datetime.timedelta(days=1)).date()
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime.combine(expected_date, datetime.time(15, 0))
    assert result == expected_dt

import pytest
from app.ai.personas import get_conversation_style, CONVERSATION_STYLES, STYLE_ALIASES

def test_none_input_returns_friendly_viewing():
    result = get_conversation_style(None)
    assert result == CONVERSATION_STYLES["friendly_viewing"]

def test_empty_string_and_whitespace_input_returns_friendly_viewing():
    assert get_conversation_style("") == CONVERSATION_STYLES["friendly_viewing"]
    assert get_conversation_style("   ") == CONVERSATION_STYLES["friendly_viewing"]

def test_exact_match_in_conversation_styles_returns_corresponding_value():
    # Pick a known key from CONVERSATION_STYLES other than "friendly_viewing"
    keys = [k for k in CONVERSATION_STYLES.keys() if k != "friendly_viewing"]
    if not keys:
        pytest.skip("No keys in CONVERSATION_STYLES other than 'friendly_viewing'")
    key = keys[0]
    assert get_conversation_style(key) == CONVERSATION_STYLES[key]
    # Also test with leading/trailing whitespace
    assert get_conversation_style(f"  {key}  ") == CONVERSATION_STYLES[key]

def test_alias_key_returns_conversation_style_for_canonical_key():
    if not STYLE_ALIASES:
        pytest.skip("STYLE_ALIASES is empty")
    alias_key = next(iter(STYLE_ALIASES.keys()))
    canonical_key = STYLE_ALIASES[alias_key]
    # canonical_key must be in CONVERSATION_STYLES per spec
    assert canonical_key in CONVERSATION_STYLES
    assert get_conversation_style(alias_key) == CONVERSATION_STYLES[canonical_key]
    # Also test with whitespace around alias
    assert get_conversation_style(f"  {alias_key}  ") == CONVERSATION_STYLES[canonical_key]

def test_unknown_style_returns_friendly_viewing():
    unknown_style = "this_style_does_not_exist"
    # Ensure unknown_style is not in CONVERSATION_STYLES or STYLE_ALIASES
    assert unknown_style not in CONVERSATION_STYLES
    assert unknown_style not in STYLE_ALIASES
    assert get_conversation_style(unknown_style) == CONVERSATION_STYLES["friendly_viewing"]

def test_case_sensitivity_of_matching():
    # Pick a known key from CONVERSATION_STYLES other than "friendly_viewing"
    keys = [k for k in CONVERSATION_STYLES.keys() if k != "friendly_viewing"]
    if not keys:
        pytest.skip("No keys in CONVERSATION_STYLES other than 'friendly_viewing'")
    key = keys[0]
    # Change case of key (upper or lower)
    altered_case = key.upper() if key.lower() == key else key.lower()
    # It should not match and fallback to friendly_viewing
    assert get_conversation_style(altered_case) == CONVERSATION_STYLES["friendly_viewing"]

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import re
from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude

# Since LANDLORD_ATTITUDES is unspecified, we define a minimal set for testing
LANDLORD_ATTITUDES = {"aggressive", "suspicious", "helpful", "friendly", "cold", "slow_reply", "responsive"}

# We patch _sender and _content indirectly by crafting messages accordingly.
# The function uses _sender(message) to check if sender is "landlord" or "inbound".
# We simulate this by monkeypatching detect_landlord_attitude._sender and _content if needed.
# But spec forbids calling private helpers directly.
# Instead, we rely on message dicts and assume _sender returns message["sender"] (or "landlord"/"inbound" keys).
# To simulate _sender, we will monkeypatch detect_landlord_attitude._sender for tests.

# Similarly, _content returns message.get("message") or message.get("content") or "".

# We monkeypatch detect_landlord_attitude._sender and _content for all tests.

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_helpers(monkeypatch):
    # Patch _sender to return message["sender"] if present, else ""
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory._sender", lambda m: m.get("sender", ""))
    # Patch _content to return message.get("message") or message.get("content") or ""
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory._content", lambda m: str(m.get("message") or m.get("content") or ""))
    yield


def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_if_valid_else_responsive():
    # No landlord messages, previous in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES
    previous = "friendly"
    messages = [{"sender": "tenant", "message": "Hello"}]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages, previous=previous) == previous

    # No landlord messages, previous not in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES
    previous = "unknown_attitude"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages, previous=previous) == "responsive"

    # messages is None, previous in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(None, previous="cold") == "cold"

    # messages empty list, previous not in LANDLORD_ATTITUDES
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="not_in_set") == "responsive"


def test_pattern_matching_priority_and_case_insensitivity():
    # aggressive pattern matched in recent_text (last 4 landlord messages)
    # We simulate patterns by including keywords that would match the patterns.
    # Since exact patterns are unspecified, we assume "angry" triggers aggressive,
    # "suspect" triggers suspicious, "help" triggers helpful, "friend" triggers friendly, "coldword" triggers cold.
    # We patch re.search to simulate pattern matching priority.

    import app.ai.conversation_memory as conv_mem

    def fake_re_search(pattern, string, flags=0):
        # aggressive patterns contain "angry"
        if "angry" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "AGGRESSIVE":
                return True
        # suspicious patterns contain "suspect"
        if "suspect" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "SUSPICIOUS":
                return True
        # helpful patterns contain "help"
        if "help" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "HELPFUL":
                return True
        # friendly patterns contain "friend"
        if "friend" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "FRIENDLY":
                return True
        # cold patterns contain "coldword"
        if "coldword" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "COLD":
                return True
        return False

    # Patch re.search to simulate pattern matching with priority order
    # We'll patch the patterns lists to contain distinct strings and patch re.search accordingly
    monkeypatch = pytest.MonkeyPatch()
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "AGGRESSIVE_PATTERNS", ["AGGRESSIVE"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS", ["SUSPICIOUS"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "HELPFUL_PATTERNS", ["HELPFUL"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "FRIENDLY_PATTERNS", ["FRIENDLY"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "COLD_PATTERNS", ["COLD"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem.re, "search", fake_re_search)

    # aggressive matched in recent_text (last 4 landlord messages)
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "I am angry about this."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Please fix it."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"

    # suspicious matched but no aggressive
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "This looks suspect."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "suspicious"

    # helpful matched but no aggressive or suspicious
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "I want to help you."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "helpful"

    # friendly matched but no aggressive, suspicious, helpful
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "You are my friend."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "friendly"

    # cold matched only on latest message content (not recent_text)
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "coldword here."},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "cold"

    monkeypatch.undo()


def test_fallback_content_key_and_sender_aliases():
    # Test that content falls back to "content" key if "message" missing
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "content": "friendly message here"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages, previous="aggressive") == "friendly" or detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "friendly" or detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "responsive"

    # Test sender aliases "landlord" and "inbound" both accepted
    messages = [
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "helpful message"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "friendly message"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "ignore me"},
    ]
    # We patch patterns to detect "helpful" and "friendly" keywords as before
    import app.ai.conversation_memory as conv_mem

    def fake_re_search(pattern, string, flags=0):
        if "helpful" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "HELPFUL":
                return True
        if "friendly" in string.lower():
            if pattern == "FRIENDLY":
                return True
        return False

    monkeypatch = pytest.MonkeyPatch()
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "AGGRESSIVE_PATTERNS", [])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS", [])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "HELPFUL_PATTERNS", ["HELPFUL"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "FRIENDLY_PATTERNS", ["FRIENDLY"])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem, "COLD_PATTERNS", [])
    monkeypatch.setattr(conv_mem.re, "search", fake_re_search)

    # The last 4 landlord messages concatenated contain both helpful and friendly,
    # aggressive > suspicious > helpful > friendly priority means helpful wins here
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "helpful"

    monkeypatch.undo()


def test_slow_reply_condition_and_time_difference():
    now = datetime.now()
    older = now - timedelta(days=2)
    recent = now - timedelta(hours=1)

    # Two landlord messages with created_at more than 24h apart → slow_reply
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": older},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hi again", "created_at": now},
    ]
    # No pattern matches, so slow_reply triggered
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "slow_reply"

    # Two landlord messages with created_at less than 24h apart → responsive
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": recent},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hi again", "created_at": now},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "responsive"

    # Only one landlord message → no slow_reply check → responsive
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": older},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "responsive"

    # Two landlord messages but one missing created_at → no slow_reply → responsive
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello", "created_at": older},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hi again"},
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "responsive"

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import re
from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude

# Since patterns and LANDLORD_ATTITUDES are unspecified, we assume some example attitudes for testing
LANDLORD_ATTITUDES = {"aggressive", "suspicious", "helpful", "friendly", "cold", "slow_reply", "responsive"}

# Helper to create message dict with sender and content keys
def make_message(sender_label, message=None, content=None, created_at=None):
    msg = {}
    if message is not None:
        msg["message"] = message
    elif content is not None:
        msg["content"] = content
    if created_at is not None:
        msg["created_at"] = created_at
    # We simulate _sender by monkeypatching detect_landlord_attitude._sender if needed,
    # but since we cannot access private helpers, we embed sender info in a special key for test
    # Instead, we rely on the spec that _sender(message) returns sender label.
    # So we add a special key "_test_sender" and monkeypatch _sender in the test.
    msg["_test_sender"] = sender_label
    return msg

# Monkeypatch _sender inside detect_landlord_attitude to use _test_sender key for testing
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_sender(monkeypatch):
    def _sender(message):
        return message.get("_test_sender", "")
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory._sender", _sender)
    # Also patch _content to use message or content keys as per spec
    def _content(message):
        return str(message.get("message") or message.get("content") or "")
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory._content", _content)
    yield

# We also need to patch the pattern lists to test pattern matching behavior
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_patterns(monkeypatch):
    # Patterns are regex strings, we define simple patterns for testing
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory.AGGRESSIVE_PATTERNS", [r"angry", r"furious"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory.SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS", [r"suspicious", r"doubt"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory.HELPFUL_PATTERNS", [r"help", r"assist"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory.FRIENDLY_PATTERNS", [r"hello", r"thanks"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.conversation_memory.COLD_PATTERNS", [r"no interest", r"ignore"])
    yield

@pytest.mark.parametrize("previous,expected", [
    (None, "responsive"),
    ("friendly", "friendly"),
    ("unknown_attitude", "responsive"),
])
def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_or_responsive(previous, expected):
    # messages is None or empty list → no landlord messages
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(None, previous=previous) == expected
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous=previous) == expected

def test_landlord_message_content_key_fallback_and_case_insensitivity():
    # landlord messages with "content" key fallback and case-insensitive pattern matching
    messages = [
        make_message("landlord", content="I am very ANGRY about this"),  # aggressive pattern in content key
        make_message("landlord", message="Hello there"),  # friendly pattern in message key
    ]
    # aggressive has priority over friendly
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"

def test_pattern_priority_and_recent_text_limit():
    # Only last 4 landlord messages considered for recent_text pattern matching
    # aggressive > suspicious > helpful > friendly > cold
    # Provide 5 landlord messages, only last 4 considered
    messages = [
        make_message("landlord", message="hello"),  # friendly, but 5th last, ignored
        make_message("landlord", message="help me"),  # helpful
        make_message("landlord", message="doubtful"),  # suspicious
        make_message("landlord", message="furious"),  # aggressive
        make_message("landlord", message="thanks"),  # friendly
    ]
    # Last 4 messages: helpful, suspicious, aggressive, friendly
    # aggressive pattern matched → "aggressive"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "aggressive"

def test_cold_pattern_matches_only_latest_message():
    # cold patterns matched only against latest landlord message content
    messages = [
        make_message("landlord", message="hello"),  # friendly pattern in earlier message
        make_message("landlord", message="No Interest in this"),  # cold pattern in latest message
    ]
    # cold pattern matched on latest message → returns "cold"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "cold"

def test_slow_reply_time_difference_check():
    now = datetime.now()
    older = now - timedelta(days=2)
    messages = [
        make_message("landlord", message="hello", created_at=older),
        make_message("landlord", message="random text", created_at=now),
    ]
    # No pattern matched, but time difference > 24h → slow_reply
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages) == "slow_reply"

def test_slow_reply_not_triggered_if_missing_created_at_or_one_message():
    now = datetime.now()
    older = now - timedelta(days=2)
    # Only one landlord message → no slow_reply
    messages_one = [
        make_message("landlord", message="hello", created_at=older),
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages_one) == "responsive"

    # Two landlord messages but missing created_at in one → no slow_reply
    messages_missing_date = [
        make_message("landlord", message="hello", created_at=older),
        make_message("landlord", message="random text"),  # no created_at
    ]
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(messages_missing_date) == "responsive"

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_none_and_empty_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_exact_matching_and_case_insensitivity():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "OUTBOUND"},
        {"sender": "Ai"},
        {"sender": "bot"},
        {"sender": "user1"},
        {"sender": "user "},
        {"sender": "user\n"},
    ]
    # Only exact matches count, ignoring case
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_fallback_to_direction_and_missing_keys():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "User"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "", "direction": "ai"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": None},
        {},
    ]
    # First four count, last two treated as empty string sender -> no count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_falsy_sender_and_direction_values():
    messages = [
        {"sender": None},
        {"sender": ""},
        {"sender": 0},
        {"direction": None},
        {"direction": ""},
        {"direction": 0},
        {},
    ]
    # All treated as empty string sender, no matches
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 0

def test_mixed_valid_and_invalid_senders():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "ai"},
        {"sender": "USER"},  # duplicate but counts
        {"sender": "UserX"},
        {"sender": "tenant123"},
        {"sender": "outbound!"},
        {"sender": "aI "},
    ]
    # Only exact matches count, so last three do not count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 5

def test_ordering_does_not_affect_count():
    messages1 = [{"sender": "user"}, {"sender": "ai"}, {"sender": "tenant"}]
    messages2 = [{"sender": "tenant"}, {"sender": "user"}, {"sender": "ai"}]
    messages3 = [{"sender": "ai"}, {"sender": "tenant"}, {"sender": "user"}]
    assert outbound_count(messages1) == 3
    assert outbound_count(messages2) == 3
    assert outbound_count(messages3) == 3

import datetime
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_empty_messages_returns_none():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_fallback_to_content_key_and_empty_string_for_missing_keys():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"irrelevant": "no message or content"},
        {"content": "Booked for 3pm"},
        {"message": None, "content": "Discussion at 4pm"},
        {"message": "Viewing at 5pm"},
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages considered, all here.
    # Last qualifying candidate is from last message: "Viewing at 5pm"
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 17, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_considers_only_last_8_messages_in_order():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = []
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered
    for i in range(10):
        if i == 1:
            messages.append({"message": "Booked for 3pm"})  # ignored (9th from last)
        elif i == 3:
            messages.append({"message": "Booked for 4pm"})  # ignored (7th from last)
        elif i == 5:
            messages.append({"message": "Booked for 5pm"})  # considered (5th from last)
        else:
            messages.append({"message": f"Message {i}"})
    # last qualifying candidate is from message at index 9 (last message)
    messages[-1] = {"message": "Viewing at 6pm"}
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 18, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected_dt

def test_time_suffix_and_hour_adjustments():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    # Test pm suffix with hour < 12
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # Test am suffix with hour == 12
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 12am"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 0, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # Test no suffix with hour in 1..7 (assumed pm)
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 4"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 16, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

def test_target_date_parsing_keywords_and_numeric_date():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)  # Saturday
    # "day after tomorrow" -> now + 2 days = 2024-06-03
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm day after tomorrow"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 3, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # "tomorrow" -> now + 1 day = 2024-06-02
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm tomorrow"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 2, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # "today" -> now date = 2024-06-01
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm today"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # numeric date with day/month (no year), date in future this year
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm on 5/6"}]  # 5 June 2024
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # numeric date with day/month (no year), date in past, year incremented
    now_late = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 10, 12, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm on 5/6"}]  # 5 June < now date
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2025, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now_late) == expected_dt

    # weekday name "monday" after saturday 2024-06-01 is 2024-06-03
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm monday"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 3, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

def test_candidate_datetime_before_now_rolls_forward_one_day():
    now = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 15, 0)
    # Time parsed is 3pm today, which equals now, so no roll forward
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 3pm today"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 1, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

    # Time parsed is 2pm today, which is before now, so roll forward one day
    messages = [{"message": "Booked for 2pm today"}]
    expected_dt = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 2, 14, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected_dt

import re
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import detect_stage, VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED

def test_empty_and_none_input_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None

def test_latest_message_rearrange_triggers_viewing_discussion():
    messages = [{"message": "Can we rearrange the appointment?"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_negating_pattern_triggers_viewing_discussion(monkeypatch):
    # Patch NEGATING_PATTERNS to include a simple pattern for testing
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.NEGATING_PATTERNS", [r"nope"])
    messages = [{"message": "Nope, I don't want that"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_latest_message_fallback_to_content_key_and_case_insensitive():
    messages = [{"content": "I want to RESCHEDULE this"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_only_last_8_messages_considered_and_order_preserved(monkeypatch):
    # Patch NEGATING_PATTERNS to include a pattern that matches "cancel"
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.NEGATING_PATTERNS", [r"cancel"])
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered, latest message does not match negation
    messages = [{"message": "ignore"}]*2 + [{"message": "cancel"}] + [{"message": "ignore"}]*6
    # The negation is at index 2 (3rd message), outside last 8, so should not trigger
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None
    # Now put negation in last message
    messages = [{"message": "ignore"}]*9 + [{"message": "cancel"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages[-8:]) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

def test_booking_context_messages_trigger_viewing_booked(monkeypatch):
    # Patch BOOKED_PATTERNS and _has_time to simulate booking detection
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.BOOKED_PATTERNS", [r"confirmed"])
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages._has_time", lambda text: "10am" in text)
    messages = [
        {"message": "Your appointment is confirmed at 10am"},
        {"message": "Looking forward to it"},
        {"message": "confirmed"},
        {"message": "See you then at 10am"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

def test_combined_recent_messages_matching_discussion_patterns_triggers_viewing_discussion(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.ai.stages.DISCUSSION_PATTERNS", [r"question"])
    messages = [
        {"message": "I have a question about the booking"},
        {"message": "Please help"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages

def test_none_input_returns_empty_list():
    assert landlord_messages(None) == []

def test_empty_input_returns_empty_list():
    assert landlord_messages([]) == []

def test_filters_by_sender_key_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "LandLord"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND"},
        {"sender": "other"},
        {"sender": ""},
        {"sender": None},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "LandLord"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_filters_by_direction_key_when_sender_missing_or_falsy():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "INBOUND"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"direction": "OUTBOUND"},
        {"direction": ""},
        {"direction": None},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "", "direction": "INBOUND"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "direction": "landlord"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"direction": "INBOUND"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "", "direction": "INBOUND"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_excludes_messages_with_no_sender_or_direction_or_falsy_values():
    messages = [
        {},
        {"sender": ""},
        {"direction": ""},
        {"sender": None},
        {"direction": None},
        {"sender": False},
        {"direction": 0},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == []

def test_preserves_order_and_returns_original_dict_references():
    m1 = {"sender": "landlord"}
    m2 = {"direction": "INBOUND"}
    m3 = {"sender": "tenant"}
    input_messages = [m1, m2, m3]
    result = landlord_messages(input_messages)
    assert result == [m1, m2]
    assert result[0] is m1
    assert result[1] is m2
